AI&I for Twist To AXIS

AI&I · PRODUCTION REEL

Twist. Reveal.
Repeat until it's right.

Before any of this reaches TikTok, every AXIS video gets tested, watched frame-by-frame, and refined — the same instant color-change reveal that makes your product work is the standard we're holding the footage to.

Prepared for Janice Johnson · Twist To AXIS · Take-by-Take Production Notes

Here's exactly how we got the AXIS video suite to where it is — three formats, seven takes, and the real notes from each round: what didn't work, what we changed, and why. Not just polished final cuts, but the actual process behind them.

U

Format One

✓ Locked & Approved

UGC — Gym Reaction

The reaction-content format — a real person, mid-workout, twisting the cap and reacting the way your actual TikTok customers do. This is now our gold-standard technique reference: every lesson learned here (grip, pronunciation, sip timing) carries over into every other format.

1Take One
Early Pass
⚠ what we caught

Bottle grip and scale needed a truer read — we're aiming for the exact way people actually hold a 2oz shot in real reaction videos, full hand around the body, not a pinched grip that reads too small on camera.

Dialogue pacing was rushed — she carried on speaking before finishing the sip, instead of the natural beat-then-continue rhythm real reactions have.

↓ Fix applied: full-hand grip at true 2oz scale + explicit pause beat after the sip before dialogue continues.
2Take Two
✓ Approved — Final
✓ what landed

Correct scale and natural full-hand grip, milligram dosages spoken clearly in full ("thirty milligrams," not abbreviated), and a believable sip-then-pause before she keeps talking.

This is the version we're shipping — and the technique baseline for every format below.

Production stills — UGC
01 / 03
G

Format Two

✓ Locked & Approved

Giant Figure — Floor Discovery

A bigger, more cinematic beat: the bottle sits on the floor at true scale, she turns, notices it, walks over, and twists the cap. The whole format hinges on one thing — does the reaction feel involuntary and real, the way it does in your actual customer footage?

1Take One
Liked, Not Final
⚠ what we caught

The middle-to-end performance was genuinely good — but the dialogue wasn't locked to a scripted line, so the model improvised the gesture: she pointed at and labeled the wrong bottle as "the real one," a mismatch between what she said and what she pointed to.

↓ Fix applied: dialogue scripted verbatim so the line and the gesture can't drift apart again.
2Take Two
✓ Approved — Final
✓ what landed

She turns, walks to the bottle, twists the cap, and the color-change reaction reads as genuinely startled — close to the real, involuntary reaction your TikTok audience already knows.

Kept as final as-is. A small dialogue/pointing quirk carried over, but the physical reaction is so strong that a redo would risk losing what makes it work — this one wasn't worth chasing further.

Production stills — Giant Figure
02 / 03
P

Format Three

◐ In Refinement

Camera POV — First-Person Reveal

The most intimate format: you're looking through her eyes as the bottle comes up and the cap twists. This one's still in active beta — we're documenting the process openly because getting the reveal exactly right is worth the extra pass, and we want you to see the standard we're holding it to.

1Take One
Two Issues Found
⚠ what we caught

"Milligrams" was mispronounced as "millicum" throughout the line — the script needs the dosage spelled out in full words, not the abbreviation.

The color change happened gradually across three separate twists instead of one instant reveal — real product behavior is closer to a seal breaking, an instant burst, not a slow fade.

↓ Fix applied: "thirty milligrams" spelled out in full + prompt language for an instant, explosive reveal on the twist.
2Take Two
Improved
◐ where this landed

Corrected script and reveal language applied. A step forward from Take One — we kept testing rather than calling it early, because the reveal is the entire hook of this format and it has to feel instant.

↓ Continued refinement on reveal timing and pacing.
3Take Three
Closest Yet
◐ where this stands

The strongest version so far — but not locked yet. One more refinement pass is planned before Camera POV joins UGC and Giant Figure as a fully approved format.

Production stills — Camera POV
03 / 03

This is what iteration looks like on our end — catching what's wrong, fixing it fast, and showing our work. That's the same energy every AXIS platform gets going forward.

— AI&I

Three formats, seven takes, documented as we go.
